q.en: According to the book, what is the largest animal on Earth?
q.zh: 根据这本书，地球上最大的动物是什么？

options.en: The whale (such as the blue whale).
The elephant.
The great white shark. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 大白鲨。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The dinosaur.

explain.en: The Introduction states 'Whales are very large. In fact, they are the largest animals on Earth... bigger than any known dinosaur.'
explain.zh: 引言写道：'鲸鱼非常大。事实上，它们是地球上最大的动物……比任何已知的恐龙都大。'

options.en: Through their mouths.
Through gills like a fish. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 像鱼一样通过鳃。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Through a blowhole on the top of their head.
Through their flippers.

explain.en: The book says whales 'breathe through a blowhole on the top of their head' and 'do not breathe through their mouths.'
explain.zh: 书中说鲸鱼'通过头顶上的喷气孔呼吸'，并且'不是用嘴呼吸'。

q.en: What is the main difference between toothed whales and baleen whales?
q.zh: 齿鲸和须鲸的主要区别是什么？

options.en: Toothed whales only live in rivers. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 齿鲸只生活在河流里。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Toothed whales have teeth; baleen whales have baleen that filters food from water.
options.zh: 齿鲸有牙齿；须鲸有须，用来从水中过滤食物。

options.en: They are exactly the same.
Baleen whales are always much larger.

explain.en: The book divides whales into 'toothed whales' (with sharp teeth) and 'baleen whales' that have baleen material to filter small sea life.
explain.zh: 书中把鲸分为'齿鲸'（有尖牙）和'须鲸'（有用来过滤小型海洋生物的鲸须）。

q.en: Why is the male narwhal unusual among whales?
q.zh: 雄性的独角鲸在鲸类中为什么很特别？

options.en: It is the smallest whale. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 它是最小的鲸。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: It has a long tusk (up to 8 feet) growing from the front of its head.
options.zh: 它头上长着一根长长的獠牙（可达8英尺）。

options.en: It has no tail.
It cannot swim.

explain.en: The book calls the narwhal 'one of the strangest whales' because 'The male narwhal has a large tusk growing straight out of the front of its head.'
explain.zh: 书中称独角鲸是'最奇怪的鲸之一'，因为'雄性独角鲸头上正前方长出一根大獠牙'。

q.en: In this book, what does the word 'blubber' mean?
q.zh: 在这本书中，'blubber'（鲸脂）这个词是什么意思？

options.en: A sound whales make.
The food whales eat.
A type of small whale. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一种小型鲸鱼。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A layer of fat found beneath the skin of whales.
options.zh: 鲸鱼皮肤下的一层脂肪。

explain.en: The glossary defines blubber as 'a layer of fat found beneath the skin of whales.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将 blubber 定义为'鲸鱼皮肤下的一层脂肪'。

q.en: How does the book help readers understand how big a blue whale is?
q.zh: 这本书是如何帮助读者理解蓝鲸有多大的？

options.en: By comparing it to school buses, a small car, and an elephant.
options.zh: 把它和学校巴士、小汽车以及大象作比较。

options.en: By showing only a picture. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 只展示一张图片。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: By saying it is small.
By using the alphabet.

explain.en: The book compares a blue whale to 'nearly as much as 20 school buses,' a heart 'as big as a small car,' and an elephant 'less than one school bus.'
explain.zh: 书中把蓝鲸与'差不多20辆校车'、'像小汽车一样大'的心脏，以及'不到一辆校车'的大象作比较。

q.en: How do dolphins and sperm whales find objects in dark or deep water?
q.zh: 海豚和抹香鲸是如何在黑暗或深水中找到物体的？

options.en: They make clicking sounds and use the returning echoes (echolocation).
options.zh: 它们发出咔哒声，并利用返回的回声（回声定位）。

options.en: They use their blowholes. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 它们用喷气孔。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: They follow other fish.
They use their eyes only.

explain.en: The book says dolphins make a clicking sound that 'bounces off objects and returns,' and 'Sperm whales also use this method when hunting.'
explain.zh: 书中说海豚发出咔哒声，'撞到物体后返回'，而'抹香鲸在捕猎时也使用这种方法'。

q.en: The book says a blue whale's sound reaches 150 decibels while a jet engine is 110 decibels. What does this comparison show?
q.zh: 书中说蓝鲸的声音达到150分贝，而喷气式发动机是110分贝。这个对比说明了什么？

options.en: Whales are completely silent.
Decibels measure length.
options.zh: 分贝是用来测量长度的。

options.en: Blue whales are extremely loud—louder than a jet engine.
options.zh: 蓝鲸非常响——比喷气式发动机还响。

options.en: Jet engines are louder than whales. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 喷气式发动机比鲸鱼更响。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book states 'A blue whale makes the loudest sound of any animal' at 150 dB, compared with a jet engine at 110 dB, so whales are louder.
explain.zh: 书中说'蓝鲸发出的声音是动物中最响的'，达150分贝，而喷气式发动机为110分贝，所以鲸鱼更响。

q.en: Why were whales hunted for many years?
q.zh: 多年来人们为什么要捕猎鲸鱼？

options.en: To make them into pets.
For their meat and blubber, which was used for lamp oil, soap, and more.
options.zh: 为了它们的肉和鲸脂，鲸脂被用来做灯油、肥皂等。

options.en: For their songs.
Because they were friendly pets. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 因为它们是友好的宠物。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says 'For years, whales were hunted for their meat and blubber. The blubber was used for lamp oil... paint, soap, and cosmetics.'
explain.zh: 书中说'多年来，鲸鱼因肉和鲸脂被猎杀。鲸脂被用来做灯油……油漆、肥皂和化妆品。'

q.en: What point does the book make about protecting whales?
q.zh: 关于保护鲸鱼，这本书提出了什么观点？

options.en: Pollution helps whales.
Whales are already completely safe. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 鲸鱼已经完全安全了。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Hunting should continue.
Whales reproduce slowly, so it will take hundreds of years to recover their numbers.
options.zh: 鲸鱼繁殖缓慢，所以要让数量恢复需要数百年。

explain.en: The book says 'whales reproduce slowly. So it will take hundreds of years to get whale populations back to safe levels.'
explain.zh: 书中说'鲸鱼繁殖缓慢。所以要让鲸鱼数量恢复到安全水平需要数百年。'

prompt.en: The book says whales are threatened by hunting and pollution, and it will take hundreds of years for their numbers to recover. What is one thing people could do to help protect whales, and why would it help?
prompt.zh: 书中说鲸鱼受到捕猎和污染的威胁，要让它们的数量恢复需要数百年。人们可以做一件什么事来帮助保护鲸鱼？为什么这样会有帮助？

guidance.en:
- Reduce ocean pollution (don't litter, keep waterways clean).
- Support laws that protect whales from hunting.
- Remember whales reproduce slowly, so protection must last a long time.
guidance.zh:
- 减少海洋污染（不乱扔垃圾、保持水道清洁）。
- 支持保护鲸鱼免遭捕猎的法律。
- 记住鲸鱼繁殖缓慢，因此保护必须长期坚持。
